calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Approving Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Dec-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Jeffrey Tachiki, Esq. appearing for Yuan-Chang "Leonard" Lo, Petitioner;
No other appearances on this matter.

The Court briefly addresses venue noting that Mr. Lo lives in Washington. Following its initial comments the Court does find venue appropriate. The Court states that it does not see the efficiency built into this trust. Mr. Tachiki states that the trust was set up for his client, that it was to last the rest of his lifetime, that it was to be kept in the family, and that it was to be held as a dynasty type property. Mr. Tachiki notes that with his client's son living in Japan that it would cause tax problems. Brief discussions are held. Mr. Tachiki states that had the decedents considered the fact that their grandson would be living in Japan that the trust wouldn't have been drafted this way. The Court notes that it seemed that the settlors had a specific plan going down two or maybe three generations. Mr. Tachiki argues that there would be loss due to the tax consequences, that his client's son resides in Japan, and that relieving the family now on the "front end" would benefit the family. The Court states that it seems like it would be setting up a sub-trust, that it could be simpler with a blocked account with withdrawals, and that it seems that this is veering from the settlors' intent. Further discussions are held. Mr. Tachiki confirms for the Court that he is not aware of any objections to the petition. The Court notes that it is inclined to grant the petition.

The Petition for Order Approving Modification of Trust is taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Chan. Matter not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Modification Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-03-2021 Continued To Nov-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
